carbon_mcp_and_packages.overview: Essential guidance on using the Carbon MCP server and working with Carbon React libraries, including best practices for importing and styling components from these libraries.

ssr_detection.title: SSR and Build System Detection
ssr_detection.description: Before implementing packages with browser-only code (Web Components, certain Carbon packages), check for SSR indicators: vite.config.js with ssr config, server.js, entry-server files.

implementation_patterns.pattern: Use React.lazy() and useEffect with dynamic imports for browser-only components. Return null during SSR. Add packages to vite.config.js ssr.external array.

theme_configuration.title: Carbon Theme Configuration
theme_configuration.description: Carbon components inherit theme from the host page by default. Only use explicit theme configuration when the host does NOT use Carbon.

rules.rule: Check if host application uses Carbon before setting explicit themes
Omit theme config when host has theme management or uses @carbon/react
Use explicit theme properties only for non-Carbon applications

detection.indicator: Theme context or components in project

carbon_mcp.title: Carbon MCP Server
carbon_mcp.description: The Carbon MCP server provides tools for searching Carbon Design System documentation and code examples. Use these tools to research components, patterns, and implementation approaches.

when_to_use.scenario: When researching Carbon AI Chat documentation (APIs, types, migration guides)
When researching component props and usage
When checking for best practices in code
When looking for Carbon AI Chat code examples and sample applications

strategy.name: Component Research
strategy.description: When implementing a specific component, search for its documentation and examples

strategy.name: Pattern Research
strategy.description: When implementing a common UI pattern, search for relevant guidance

strategy.name: AI Chat Documentation Research
strategy.description: When implementing Carbon AI Chat features, search for API documentation, types, and migration guides

guidelines.guideline: Do not set filters.component_type, filters.component_id, or filters.ibm_products for AI Chat docs
Query directly with API/type names or migration topics
For upgrade/migration queries, include "migration-1.0.0" in the query

strategy.name: AI Chat Code Examples Research
strategy.description: When implementing Carbon AI Chat features, search for code examples and sample applications

example_queries.query: ai chat web components custom element

guidelines.guideline: ALWAYS set filters.component_type to "React" or "Web Components" based on user's framework
Do not set filters.ibm_products for AI Chat examples
Include "ai chat" phrase in query for intent targeting
Include example root (basic, custom-element, history, watsonx, watch-state) when known
For specific file retrieval, include filename in query (e.g., "customLoadHistory.ts")
Server automatically provides complete files even if they span multiple chunks

carbon_packages.title: Carbon React Libraries
carbon_packages.description: Carbon React libraries (defined as Carbon v11 packages including @carbon/react, Carbon for IBM Products, Carbon Icons, Carbon Pictograms, Carbon Charts and other Carbon React libraries) provide components for building consistent, accessible interfaces. Always use these official packages directly rather than recreating components or functionality.

component_stability.title: Component Stability and Usage Guidelines
component_stability.description: Carbon does not duplicate components. The only exceptions are components being redesigned in @carbon/labs-react before transfer to @carbon/react, or @carbon/ibm-products components moving to core Carbon.

level.name: stable
level.description: Production-ready components in Carbon React libraries

level.name: unstable_preview
level.description: Components marked as "unstable" or "preview" in @carbon/react. IBM Products sometimes refers to unstable components as "canary".
level.usage_rule: DO NOT use unstable, preview, or canary components unless explicitly instructed by the user. Once the user has instructed their use locally, you may assume they can continue to be used.
level.rationale: These components are experimental and may have breaking changes or incomplete functionality. They should only be used when the user has made an informed decision to accept the risks.

level.name: labs
level.description: Experimental components in @carbon/labs-react being developed or redesigned before potential transfer to @carbon/react
level.usage_rule: DO NOT use @carbon/labs-react components unless explicitly instructed by the user. Once the user has instructed their use locally, you may assume they can continue to be used.
level.rationale: Labs components are experimental and undergoing active development. They may have significant API changes or be deprecated without notice.

step.question: Is there a stable component in Carbon React libraries that meets the requirement?
step.action: Use the stable component from Carbon React libraries

step.question: Has the user explicitly instructed use of an unstable/preview/canary/labs component?

if_yes.action: Use the component as instructed and document its experimental status

if_no.action: Do not use experimental components; propose stable alternatives or custom implementation

package.name: @carbon/react
package.description: Core Carbon React components library. This is the main package for Carbon React libraries.
package.import_example: import { Button, Tile, Grid, Column } from '@carbon/react';

package.name: @carbon/icons-react
package.description: Carbon icons library for React. Provides a comprehensive set of icons.

package.name: @carbon/charts
package.description: Carbon charts library. Provides data visualization components.

package.name: @carbon/ibm-products
package.description: Extended components for IBM products, building on Carbon components. This package provides additional, more complex components that are commonly used in IBM products.

package.name: @carbon/pictograms-react
package.description: Carbon pictograms library for React. Provides larger, more detailed illustrations.

best_practices.title: Best Practices for Using Carbon React Libraries

practice.name: Use Official Components
practice.description: Always use official components from Carbon React libraries rather than recreating them. This ensures consistency, accessibility, and maintainability.

practice.name: Add Required Dependencies
practice.description: When a component from a Carbon React library is needed but the package is not in the project, add the dependency rather than recreating the component. Carbon does not duplicate components, so always prefer using the official package.
practice.important_note: Do not recreate components that exist in Carbon React libraries. Always add the required package and use the official component to ensure consistency, accessibility, and maintainability.

practice.name: Add Required Styling
practice.description: When importing new Carbon React libraries, ensure you also add the required styling.

practice.name: Use MCP for Research
practice.description: Use the Carbon MCP server to research components and patterns before implementation. This ensures you follow best practices and leverage existing solutions.
